title: The Language of Dust
text_zh: 搬进新公寓以后，我渐渐学会了阅读灰尘。每个角落的灰尘都在讲述一段关于空气流动的故事。窗台上的灰最厚，因为那里是外界微粒进入室内的第一站，像海关一样接纳了来自城市的无数旅客。书架顶部的灰尘则呈现一种均匀的灰白色薄膜，说明那里的空气几乎是静止的，没有人类活动的扰动。最有趣的是厨房抽油烟机上方的灰尘——它带着微微的黄色，混合了油脂和水蒸气，形成了一种黏性的沉积物，记录着每一顿饭的烹饪方式。我用手指在不同表面轻轻划过，灰尘的质地各不相同：有的干燥疏松如细沙，有的潮湿绵密如泥土。每隔两周我会彻底打扫一次，然后观察灰尘重新聚集的过程。它总是先出现在角落和缝隙里，仿佛那些被遗忘的空间对微粒有一种特殊的引力。灰尘是沉默的编年史家，它忠实地记录着一个空间里发生的一切——只要你愿意俯下身去阅读。
text_pinyin: Bān jìn xīn gōngyù yǐhòu, wǒ jiànjiàn xuéhuì le yuèdú huīchén. Měi gè jiǎoluò de huīchén dōu zài jiǎngshù yí duàn guānyú kōngqì liúdòng de gùshi. Chuāngtái shàng de huī zuì hòu, yīnwèi nàlǐ shì wàijiè wēilì jìnrù shìnèi de dì yī zhàn, xiàng hǎiguān yíyàng jiēnà le láizì chéngshì de wúshù lǚkè. Shūjià dǐngbù de huīchén zé chéngxiàn yì zhǒng jūnyún de huībáisè bómó, shuōmíng nàlǐ de kōngqì jīhū shì jìngzhǐ de, méiyǒu rénlèi huódòng de rǎodòng. Zuì yǒuqù de shì chúfáng chōu yóuyānjī shàngfāng de huīchén — tā dài zhe wēiwēi de huángsè, hùnhé le yóuzhī hé shuǐzhēngqì, xíngchéng le yì zhǒng niánxìng de chénjīwù, jìlù zhe měi yí dùn fàn de pēngrèn fāngshì. Wǒ yòng shǒuzhǐ zài bùtóng biǎomiàn qīngqīng huà guò, huīchén de zhìdì gè bù xiāngtóng: yǒu de gānzào shūsōng rú xì shā, yǒu de cháoshī miánmì rú nítǔ. Měi gé liǎng zhōu wǒ huì chèdǐ dǎsǎo yí cì, ránhòu guānchá huīchén chóngxīn jùjí de guòchéng. Tā zǒng shì xiān chūxiàn zài jiǎoluò hé fèngxì lǐ, fǎngfú nàxiē bèi yíwàng de kōngjiān duì wēilì yǒu yì zhǒng tèshū de yǐnlì. Huīchén shì chénmò de biānniánshǐ jiā, tā zhōngshí de jìlù zhe yí gè kōngjiān lǐ fāshēng de yíqiè — zhǐyào nǐ yuànyì fǔ xià shēn qù yuèdú.
text_en: After moving into the new apartment, I gradually learned to read dust. The dust in every corner tells a story about air flow. The dust on the windowsill is thickest, because that's the first stop for outside particles entering the room, accepting countless travelers from the city like a customs checkpoint. The dust atop the bookshelf presents an even grayish-white film, indicating the air there is nearly still, undisturbed by human activity. Most interesting is the dust above the kitchen range hood — it carries a slight yellow tint, mixed with grease and steam, forming a sticky deposit that records the cooking method of every meal. I run my finger lightly across different surfaces and the texture of dust varies: some dry and loose like fine sand, some moist and dense like earth. Every two weeks I do a thorough cleaning, then observe the process of dust regathering. It always appears first in corners and crevices, as if those forgotten spaces exert a special gravity on particles. Dust is a silent chronicler, faithfully recording everything that happens in a space — if only you're willing to bend down and read.

questions.q_zh: 作者觉得哪里的灰尘最有趣？
questions.q_en: Where does the author find the most interesting dust?

questions.q_zh: 打扫以后，灰尘首先在哪里重新出现？
questions.q_en: After cleaning, where does dust first reappear?

title: The Memory of a Wooden Bench
text_zh: 公园里有一把老木椅，椅面已经被无数人的坐姿磨出了一个浅浅的凹陷。我第一次注意到它是因为那个凹陷的形状异常规则——不是圆形的，而是一个微微偏左的椭圆，说明大多数坐在这里的人都习惯把重心放在左侧。椅背上有几道平行的划痕，间距均匀，像是有人长期把背包挂在椅背上摩擦留下的。扶手的两端磨损程度也不对称：右边的扶手比左边光滑得多，暗示着人们起身时更多地依赖右手撑扶手的力量。木材的纹理在这些磨损处变得格外清晰，就像河床在水流冲刷下暴露出底层的岩石。一个阴天的下午，我坐在这把椅子上待了整整三个小时。期间有六个人先后坐过——一对中年夫妇分享了半块面包，一个年轻女孩看了四十分钟的书，一个老人闭着眼睛晒了一会儿太阳。他们都不知道自己正在为这把椅子添加新的记忆层。木头是有耐心的，它用几十年的时间来记录人类短暂的停留。
text_pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu yì bǎ lǎo mù yǐ, yǐ miàn yǐjīng bèi wúshù rén de zuòzī mó chū le yí gè qiǎnqiǎn de āoxiàn. Wǒ dì yī cì zhùyì dào tā shì yīnwèi nàge āoxiàn de xíngzhuàng yìcháng guīzé — bú shì yuánxíng de, ér shì yí gè wēiwēi piān zuǒ de tuǒyuán, shuōmíng dà duōshù zuò zài zhèlǐ de rén dōu xíguàn bǎ zhòngxīn fàng zài zuǒcè. Yǐ bèi shàng yǒu jǐ dào píngxíng de huàhén, jiānjù jūnyún, xiàng shì yǒu rén chángqī bǎ bēibāo guà zài yǐ bèi shàng mócā liú xià de. Fúshǒu de liǎng duān mósǔn chéngdù yě bú duìchèn: yòubiān de fúshǒu bǐ zuǒbiān guānghuá de duō, ànshì zhe rénmen qǐshēn shí gèng duō de yīlài yòushǒu chēng fúshǒu de lìliàng. Mùcái de wénlǐ zài zhèxiē mósǔn chù biàn de géwài qīngxī, jiù xiàng héchuáng zài shuǐliú chōngshuā xià bàolù chū dǐcéng de yánshí. Yí gè yīn tiān de xiàwǔ, wǒ zuò zài zhè bǎ yǐzi shàng dāi le zhěngzhěng sān gè xiǎoshí. Qījiān yǒu liù gè rén xiānhòu zuò guò — yí duì zhōngnián fūfù fēnxiǎng le bàn kuài miànbāo, yí gè niánqīng nǚhái kàn le sìshí fēnzhōng de shū, yí gè lǎorén bì zhe yǎnjīng shài le yíhuìr tàiyáng. Tāmen dōu bù zhīdào zìjǐ zhèngzài wèi zhè bǎ yǐzi tiānjiā xīn de jìyì céng. Mùtou shì yǒu nàixīn de, tā yòng jǐ shí nián de shíjiān lái jìlù rénlèi duǎnzàn de tíngliú.
text_en: There's an old wooden bench in the park whose seat has been worn into a shallow depression by countless people's sitting postures. I first noticed it because the depression was unusually regular in shape — not circular, but a slightly left-leaning oval, suggesting most people who sit here habitually shift their weight to the left side. The backrest bears several parallel scratches, evenly spaced, as if someone long hung a backpack there, leaving friction marks. The wear on the two armrests is also asymmetric: the right armrest is much smoother than the left, hinting that people rely more on their right hand pushing against the armrest when standing up. The wood grain at these worn spots has become exceptionally clear, like a riverbed exposing underlying rock after water erosion. On an overcast afternoon, I sat on this bench for a full three hours. During that time six people sat down in succession — a middle-aged couple shared half a piece of bread, a young girl read for forty minutes, an old man closed his eyes and basked in the sun for a while. None of them knew they were adding new memory layers to this bench. Wood is patient — it uses decades to record humanity's brief pauses.

questions.q_zh: 椅面凹陷的形状说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the shape of the seat depression suggest?

options.text: 大多数人把重心放在右侧
options.pinyin: dà duōshù rén bǎ zhòngxīn fàng zài yòucè
options.text_en: Most people shift their weight to the right

options.text: 大多数人把重心放在左侧
options.pinyin: dà duōshù rén bǎ zhòngxīn fàng zài zuǒcè
options.text_en: Most people shift their weight to the left

options.pinyin: yǐzi de mùcái zhìliàng bù hǎo
options.text_en: The wood quality of the bench is poor

options.text: 只有一个人经常坐这把椅子
options.pinyin: zhǐ yǒu yí gè rén jīngcháng zuò zhè bǎ yǐzi
options.text_en: Only one person regularly sits on this bench

questions.q_zh: 作者那天下午观察到几个人坐过这把椅子？
questions.q_en: How many people did the author observe sitting on the bench that afternoon?

title: The Seasons of a Stairwell
text_zh: 我住的这栋楼有六层，没有电梯。每天爬楼梯的过程中，我发现楼梯间其实有自己的四季。春天的时候，二楼张阿姨家的窗户总是开着，楼梯间会飘进一股淡淡的槐花香气，和水泥墙壁的冷硬气息形成奇妙的对比。夏天最为明显：楼梯间变成了一个天然的烟囱效应装置，热空气从底层往上升腾，顶楼的温度比一楼高出至少五度。我曾经在不同楼层放置温度计，绘制了一张纵向的温度分布图。秋天的楼梯间弥漫着各家厨房飘出的炖肉和煮汤的气味，这些气味沿着楼梯盘旋上升，在三楼的拐角处交汇融合，形成一种复杂的嗅觉和声。冬天的楼梯间最安静，水泥台阶上有时会结一层薄霜，脚踩上去发出细碎的「咯吱」声。暖气管道贴着楼梯间的墙壁延伸，偶尔发出金属膨胀的轻响，像是这栋老楼在寒冷中深深地叹了一口气。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù de zhè dòng lóu yǒu liù céng, méiyǒu diàntī. Měi tiān pá lóutī de guòchéng zhōng, wǒ fāxiàn lóutī jiān qíshí yǒu zìjǐ de sìjì. Chūntiān de shíhou, èr lóu Zhāng āyí jiā de chuānghù zǒng shì kāi zhe, lóutī jiān huì piāo jìn yì gǔ dàndàn de huáihuā xiāngqì, hé shuǐní qiángbì de lěng yìng qìxī xíngchéng qímiào de duìbǐ. Xiàtiān zuìwéi míngxiǎn: lóutī jiān biàn chéng le yí gè tiānrán de yāncōng xiàoyìng zhuāngzhì, rè kōngqì cóng dǐcéng wǎng shàng shēngténg, dǐng lóu de wēndù bǐ yī lóu gāo chū zhìshǎo wǔ dù. Wǒ céngjīng zài bùtóng lóucéng fàngzhì wēndùjì, huìzhì le yì zhāng zòngxiàng de wēndù fēnbù tú. Qiūtiān de lóutī jiān mímàn zhe gè jiā chúfáng piāo chū de dùn ròu hé zhǔ tāng de qìwèi, zhèxiē qìwèi yán zhe lóutī pánxuán shàngshēng, zài sān lóu de guǎijiǎo chù jiāohuì rónghé, xíngchéng yì zhǒng fùzá de xiùjué héshēng. Dōngtiān de lóutī jiān zuì ānjìng, shuǐní táijiē shàng yǒushí huì jié yì céng bó shuāng, jiǎo cǎi shàngqù fāchū xìsuì de 「gēzhī」 shēng. Nuǎnqì guǎndào tiē zhe lóutī jiān de qiángbì yánshēn, ǒu'ěr fāchū jīnshǔ péngzhàng de qīng xiǎng, xiàng shì zhè dòng lǎo lóu zài hánlěng zhōng shēnshēn de tàn le yì kǒu qì.
text_en: The building I live in has six floors and no elevator. During my daily stair-climbing, I've discovered that the stairwell actually has its own four seasons. In spring, Auntie Zhang on the second floor always has her window open, and a faint fragrance of locust blossoms drifts into the stairwell, forming a wonderful contrast with the cold, hard scent of concrete walls. Summer is most obvious: the stairwell becomes a natural chimney effect device, with hot air rising from the ground floor, making the top floor at least five degrees warmer than the first. I once placed thermometers on different floors and drew a vertical temperature distribution chart. In autumn, the stairwell fills with the aromas of stewed meat and simmering soup drifting from each family's kitchen — these scents spiral upward along the stairs, converging and blending at the third-floor landing into a complex olfactory chord. The stairwell is quietest in winter; sometimes a thin frost forms on the concrete steps, making a faint crunching sound underfoot. Heating pipes extend along the stairwell walls, occasionally emitting the light sound of metal expansion, as if the old building were sighing deeply in the cold.

questions.q_zh: 夏天楼梯间里发生了什么现象？
questions.q_en: What phenomenon occurs in the stairwell during summer?

options.pinyin: lóutī jiān biàn de fēicháng lěng

options.text: 热空气上升，顶楼比一楼高出五度以上
options.pinyin: rè kōngqì shàngshēng, dǐng lóu bǐ yī lóu gāo chū wǔ dù yǐshàng
options.text_en: Hot air rises, top floor is at least 5 degrees warmer
Every floor has the same temperature

questions.q_zh: 冬天暖气管道的声音被比作什么？
questions.q_en: What is the winter heating pipe sound compared to?

options.pinyin: lǎo lóu zài hánlěng zhōng tànqì
options.text_en: The old building sighing in the cold

title: Mapping Shadows
text_zh: 有一段时间我迷上了观察建筑物的影子。每天下午三点，我会站在同一个路口，记录周围建筑投射在地面上的影子轮廓。一个月以后，我积累了三十张影子地图，叠放在一起就能清楚地看到影子随季节迁移的轨迹。冬天的影子又长又窄，像是建筑物伸出的细长手臂，试图触摸远处的地面。夏天的影子则缩短为建筑物脚下的一小片暗影，仿佛它们不愿意离开自己的身体太远。最让我感到惊讶的是，某些影子区域在一天之中从未被阳光照射过——它们是永恒的阴影地带，潮湿而阴凉，催生了独特的微生态：墙角的苔藓、缝隙中的蕨类植物，甚至有几只只在阴影中活动的猫。我把这些常年不见阳光的区域称为「影子群岛」，它们像散落在城市光明海洋中的暗色小岛。一个晴朗的冬日午后，我发现两栋楼的影子在街道中央短暂地交叉重叠，形成了一个更深的暗影——那是两栋楼唯一的一次「握手」，每天只持续大约十五分钟。
text_pinyin: Yǒu yí duàn shíjiān wǒ mí shàng le guānchá jiànzhùwù de yǐngzi. Měi tiān xiàwǔ sān diǎn, wǒ huì zhàn zài tóng yí gè lùkǒu, jìlù zhōuwéi jiànzhù tóushè zài dìmiàn shàng de yǐngzi lúnkuò. Yí gè yuè yǐhòu, wǒ jīlěi le sānshí zhāng yǐngzi dìtú, dié fàng zài yìqǐ jiù néng qīngchǔ de kàn dào yǐngzi suí jìjié qiānyí de guǐjì. Dōngtiān de yǐngzi yòu cháng yòu zhǎi, xiàng shì jiànzhùwù shēn chū de xìcháng shǒubì, shìtú chùmō yuǎnchù de dìmiàn. Xiàtiān de yǐngzi zé suōduǎn wéi jiànzhùwù jiǎo xià de yì xiǎo piàn ànyǐng, fǎngfú tāmen bú yuànyì líkāi zìjǐ de shēntǐ tài yuǎn. Zuì ràng wǒ gǎndào jīngyà de shì, mǒu xiē yǐngzi qūyù zài yì tiān zhī zhōng cóngwèi bèi yángguāng zhàoshè guò — tāmen shì yǒnghéng de yīnyǐng dìdài, cháoshī ér yīnliáng, cuīshēng le dútè de wēi shēngtài: qiáng jiǎo de táixiǎn, fèngxì zhōng de juélèi zhíwù, shènzhì yǒu jǐ zhī zhǐ zài yīnyǐng zhōng huódòng de māo. Wǒ bǎ zhèxiē chángnián bú jiàn yángguāng de qūyù chēng wéi 「yǐngzi qúndǎo」, tāmen xiàng sànluò zài chéngshì guāngmíng hǎiyáng zhōng de ànsè xiǎo dǎo. Yí gè qínglǎng de dōngrì wǔhòu, wǒ fāxiàn liǎng dòng lóu de yǐngzi zài jiēdào zhōngyāng duǎnzàn de jiāochā chóngdié, xíngchéng le yí gè gèng shēn de ànyǐng — nà shì liǎng dòng lóu wéiyī de yí cì 「wòshǒu」, měi tiān zhǐ chíxù dàyuē shíwǔ fēnzhōng.
text_en: For a while I became fascinated with observing building shadows. Every day at three in the afternoon, I would stand at the same intersection and record the shadow outlines that surrounding buildings cast on the ground. After a month I had accumulated thirty shadow maps; stacked together, you could clearly see the trajectory of shadows migrating with the seasons. Winter shadows are long and narrow, like slender arms extended by buildings trying to touch the distant ground. Summer shadows shrink to a small patch of darkness at the buildings' feet, as if reluctant to stray too far from their own bodies. What surprised me most was that certain shadow zones were never touched by sunlight throughout the entire day — they are eternal shade zones, damp and cool, nurturing unique micro-ecosystems: moss in wall corners, ferns in crevices, even a few cats that only move about in the shadows. I call these perpetually sunless zones 'shadow archipelagos' — dark little islands scattered in the city's ocean of light. On a clear winter afternoon, I discovered two buildings' shadows briefly crossed and overlapped in the middle of the street, forming an even deeper darkness — the only 'handshake' between the two buildings, lasting only about fifteen minutes each day.

questions.q_zh: 作者怎么称呼那些常年没有阳光的区域？
questions.q_en: What does the author call the perpetually sunless zones?

questions.q_zh: 两栋楼影子的「握手」每天持续多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long does the two buildings' shadow 'handshake' last each day?

title: The Sound Map of a Rainy Night
text_zh: 下雨的夜晚躺在床上，我开始辨认每一种雨声的来源和质地。雨打在铁皮雨棚上是一种急促的「噼里啪啦」，节奏密集而缺乏旋律，像一群没有指挥的鼓手同时演奏。雨打在玻璃窗上则柔和得多，发出一种沉闷的「嗒嗒」声，每一滴雨都像一个微小的拳头在礼貌地敲门。阳台上的花盆积了水以后，新落下的雨滴会激起细小的水花，发出一种清脆的高音，和铁皮的低沉形成了音域上的互补。最远处传来的是雨水汇入下水道的哗哗声，那是所有雨滴最终汇聚的终点，像是一条隐形的河流在城市的地下奔涌。偶尔有一辆汽车驶过积水的路面，轮胎溅起水花的声音像是一把巨大的刷子在画布上横扫而过。我闭着眼睛，试着在脑海中绘制一幅声音地图：近处的玻璃窗、中距离的铁皮棚、远处的下水道，它们构成了一个由近及远的声学景观。雨夜的城市并不沉默，它只是换了一种语言在说话。
text_pinyin: Xià yǔ de yèwǎn tǎng zài chuáng shàng, wǒ kāishǐ biànrèn měi yì zhǒng yǔ shēng de láiyuán hé zhìdì. Yǔ dǎ zài tiěpí yǔpéng shàng shì yì zhǒng jícù de 「pīlipālā」, jiézòu mìjí ér quēfá xuánlǜ, xiàng yì qún méiyǒu zhǐhuī de gǔshǒu tóngshí yǎnzòu. Yǔ dǎ zài bōli chuāng shàng zé róuhé de duō, fāchū yì zhǒng chénmèn de 「dādā」 shēng, měi yì dī yǔ dōu xiàng yí gè wēixiǎo de quántou zài lǐmào de qiāo mén. Yángtái shàng de huāpén jī le shuǐ yǐhòu, xīn luò xià de yǔdī huì jī qǐ xìxiǎo de shuǐhuā, fāchū yì zhǒng qīngcuì de gāoyīn, hé tiěpí de dīchén xíngchéng le yīnyù shàng de hùbǔ. Zuì yuǎnchù chuán lái de shì yǔshuǐ huì rù xiàshuǐdào de huāhuā shēng, nà shì suǒyǒu yǔdī zuìzhōng huìjù de zhōngdiǎn, xiàng shì yì tiáo yǐnxíng de héliú zài chéngshì de dìxià bēnyǒng. Ǒu'ěr yǒu yí liàng qìchē shǐ guò jīshuǐ de lùmiàn, lúntāi jiàn qǐ shuǐhuā de shēngyīn xiàng shì yì bǎ jùdà de shuāzi zài huàbù shàng héng sǎo ér guò. Wǒ bì zhe yǎnjīng, shì zhe zài nǎohǎi zhōng huìzhì yì fú shēngyīn dìtú: jìnchù de bōli chuāng, zhōng jùlí de tiěpí péng, yuǎnchù de xiàshuǐdào, tāmen gòuchéng le yí gè yóu jìn jí yuǎn de shēngxué jǐngguān. Yǔ yè de chéngshì bìng bù chénmò, tā zhǐ shì huàn le yì zhǒng yǔyán zài shuōhuà.
text_en: Lying in bed on a rainy night, I begin distinguishing the source and texture of every type of rain sound. Rain on the tin awning is a rapid pitter-patter, rhythmically dense but lacking melody, like a group of drummers playing simultaneously without a conductor. Rain on the glass window is much softer, making a muffled tapping, each raindrop like a tiny fist knocking politely at a door. After water accumulates in the balcony flowerpots, newly falling drops splash tiny sprays of water, producing a crisp high pitch that complements the deep tone of the tin in register. From the farthest distance comes the rushing sound of rainwater flowing into storm drains — the final destination where all raindrops converge, like an invisible river surging beneath the city. Occasionally a car drives over a waterlogged road, and the sound of tires splashing water is like a giant brush sweeping across a canvas. With eyes closed, I try to draw a sound map in my mind: the nearby glass windows, the mid-distance tin awning, the distant storm drains — they compose an acoustic landscape stretching from near to far. A rainy city is not silent; it has merely switched to a different language.

questions.q_zh: 雨打在玻璃窗上的声音被比作什么？
questions.q_en: What is the sound of rain on glass compared to?

options.text: 微小的拳头在礼貌地敲门
options.pinyin: wēixiǎo de quántou zài lǐmào de qiāo mén
options.text_en: A tiny fist politely knocking at a door

options.pinyin: shuāzi zài huàbù shàng héng sǎo

questions.q_zh: 作者认为雨夜的城市怎样？
questions.q_en: What does the author think about a rainy city at night?

options.pinyin: huàn le yì zhǒng yǔyán zài shuōhuà

title: The Patience of a Street Lamp
text_zh: 我家楼下有一盏老式路灯，每天傍晚六点二十三分准时亮起。我之所以知道这么精确的时间，是因为我连续记录了两个月，发现它的启动时间完全由光敏传感器控制，与日落时间精确同步。这盏路灯照亮的范围是一个直径大约八米的圆形光圈，光的边缘并不清晰，而是渐渐过渡到黑暗中，像是水彩画中颜色的晕染。每天晚上，这个光圈就像一个小型剧场的舞台灯光，照亮了一系列微小的夜间演出：九点左右，一只橘色的猫会穿过光圈，它总是沿着光与影的边界行走，似乎在审慎地权衡暴露与隐蔽的利弊。十点半，遛狗的王叔叔会在灯下停留几分钟，等他的老年金毛犬慢慢嗅完电线杆周围的气味信息。深夜一点，偶尔有晚归的年轻人在灯下掏出手机查看地图，他们的脸被屏幕的蓝光和路灯的暖光同时照亮，呈现出一种奇异的双色调。路灯从不催促任何人，也从不评判谁在什么时间经过。它只是每天准时亮起，耐心地守候着这个小小的光明领地。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā lóu xià yǒu yì zhǎn lǎoshì lùdēng, měi tiān bàngwǎn liù diǎn èrshísān fēn zhǔnshí liàng qǐ. Wǒ zhī suǒyǐ zhīdào zhème jīngquè de shíjiān, shì yīnwèi wǒ liánxù jìlù le liǎng gè yuè, fāxiàn tā de qǐdòng shíjiān wánquán yóu guāngmǐn chuángǎnqì kòngzhì, yǔ rìluò shíjiān jīngquè tóngbù. Zhè zhǎn lùdēng zhàoliàng de fànwéi shì yí gè zhíjìng dàyuē bā mǐ de yuánxíng guāngquān, guāng de biānyuán bìng bù qīngxī, ér shì jiànjiàn guòdù dào hēi'àn zhōng, xiàng shì shuǐcǎihuà zhōng yánsè de yūnrǎn. Měi tiān wǎnshàng, zhège guāngquān jiù xiàng yí gè xiǎoxíng jùchǎng de wǔtái dēngguāng, zhàoliàng le yí xìliè wēixiǎo de yèjiān yǎnchū: jiǔ diǎn zuǒyòu, yì zhī júsè de māo huì chuān guò guāngquān, tā zǒng shì yán zhe guāng yǔ yǐng de biānjiè xíngzǒu, sìhū zài shěnshèn de quánhéng bàolù yǔ yǐnbì de lìbì. Shí diǎn bàn, liú gǒu de Wáng shūshu huì zài dēng xià tíngliú jǐ fēnzhōng, děng tā de lǎonián jīnmáo quǎn mànmàn xiù wán diànxiàngān zhōuwéi de qìwèi xìnxī. Shēnyè yì diǎn, ǒu'ěr yǒu wǎn guī de niánqīng rén zài dēng xià tāo chū shǒujī chákàn dìtú, tāmen de liǎn bèi píngmù de lán guāng hé lùdēng de nuǎn guāng tóngshí zhàoliàng, chéngxiàn chū yì zhǒng qíyì de shuāng sèdiào. Lùdēng cóng bù cuīcù rènhé rén, yě cóng bú píngjuàn shéi zài shénme shíjiān jīngguò. Tā zhǐ shì měi tiān zhǔnshí liàng qǐ, nàixīn de shǒuhòu zhe zhège xiǎoxiǎo de guāngmíng lǐngdì.
text_en: There's an old-fashioned street lamp downstairs from my apartment that turns on punctually at 6:23 every evening. The reason I know such a precise time is that I recorded it continuously for two months and discovered its activation is entirely controlled by a photosensitive sensor, synchronized precisely with sunset. This lamp illuminates a circular halo roughly eight meters in diameter, its edges not sharp but gradually transitioning into darkness, like the blending of colors in a watercolor painting. Every evening this circle of light is like the stage lighting of a small theater, illuminating a series of tiny nocturnal performances: around nine o'clock, an orange cat crosses through the circle, always walking along the boundary of light and shadow, as if prudently weighing the pros and cons of exposure versus concealment. At ten-thirty, Uncle Wang walking his dog pauses under the lamp for a few minutes, waiting for his elderly golden retriever to slowly sniff through the scent information around the utility pole. At one in the morning, occasionally a young person returning home late pulls out their phone under the lamp to check a map, their face illuminated simultaneously by the screen's blue light and the lamp's warm light, presenting a strangely dual-toned effect. The street lamp never rushes anyone, nor judges who passes at what hour. It simply turns on punctually every day, patiently keeping watch over its small territory of light.

questions.q_zh: 路灯的启动时间由什么控制？
questions.q_en: What controls the street lamp's activation time?

questions.q_zh: 橘色的猫在路灯下有什么特别的行为？
questions.q_en: What special behavior does the orange cat display under the lamp?

options.text_en: Sleeps in the center of the light

options.text: 沿着光与影的边界行走
options.pinyin: yán zhe guāng yǔ yǐng de biānjiè xíngzǒu
options.text_en: Walks along the boundary of light and shadow

options.pinyin: hé Wáng shūshu de gǒu yìqǐ wán

title: The Conversation Between Walls
text_zh: 老城区有一条特别窄的巷子，两面墙壁之间的距离不到一米。我每次走过这条巷子，都会注意到两面墙上的痕迹似乎在进行一场跨越时间的对话。左边的墙是一栋民国时期的砖房，红砖已经风化成了深褐色，砖缝之间的灰浆有些地方已经脱落，露出了里面黑色的空洞。右边的墙则属于一栋八十年代的混凝土建筑，表面涂着逐渐剥落的白色石灰，下面隐约可见灰色的水泥。两面墙上都有水渍的痕迹，但流向截然不同：左边墙上的水渍从上往下蜿蜒，像一棵倒挂的树；右边墙上的水渍则是横向扩展的，像一片平铺的云层。我推测这是因为两面墙的材质和表面粗糙度不同，导致雨水在上面的流动方式也不一样。在巷子的最窄处，两面墙上各有一片颜色发绿的区域——那是雨天积水时飞溅到墙面上的水痕催生出的藻类，它们隔着不到六十厘米的距离遥遥相对，像两个素未谋面却因为相同环境而长出了相同容貌的陌生人。
text_pinyin: Lǎo chéngqū yǒu yì tiáo tèbié zhǎi de xiàngzi, liǎng miàn qiángbì zhī jiān de jùlí bú dào yì mǐ. Wǒ měi cì zǒu guò zhè tiáo xiàngzi, dōu huì zhùyì dào liǎng miàn qiáng shàng de hénjì sìhū zài jìnxíng yì chǎng kuàyuè shíjiān de duìhuà. Zuǒbiān de qiáng shì yí dòng Mínguó shíqī de zhuān fáng, hóng zhuān yǐjīng fēnghuà chéng le shēn hésè, zhuān fèng zhī jiān de huījiāng yǒuxiē dìfāng yǐjīng tuōluò, lùchū le lǐmiàn hēisè de kōngdòng. Yòubiān de qiáng zé shǔyú yí dòng bāshí niándài de hùnníngtǔ jiànzhù, biǎomiàn tú zhe zhújiàn bōluò de báisè shíhuī, xiàmiàn yǐnyuē kějiàn huīsè de shuǐní. Liǎng miàn qiáng shàng dōu yǒu shuǐzì de hénjì, dàn liúxiàng jiérán bùtóng: zuǒbiān qiáng shàng de shuǐzì cóng shàng wǎng xià wānyán, xiàng yì kē dào guà de shù; yòubiān qiáng shàng de shuǐzì zé shì héngxiàng kuòzhǎn de, xiàng yí piàn píng pū de yúncéng. Wǒ tuīcè zhè shì yīnwèi liǎng miàn qiáng de cáizhì hé biǎomiàn cūcāodù bùtóng, dǎozhì yǔshuǐ zài shàngmiàn de liúdòng fāngshì yě bù yíyàng. Zài xiàngzi de zuì zhǎi chù, liǎng miàn qiáng shàng gè yǒu yí piàn yánsè fā lǜ de qūyù — nà shì yǔ tiān jīshuǐ shí fēijiàn dào qiáng miàn shàng de shuǐhén cuīshēng chū de zǎolèi, tāmen gé zhe bú dào liùshí límǐ de jùlí yáoyáo xiāngduì, xiàng liǎng gè sù wèi móumiàn què yīnwèi xiāngtóng huánjìng ér zhǎng chū le xiāngtóng róngmào de mòshēngrén.
text_en: In the old city there's a particularly narrow alley, with less than a meter between the two walls. Every time I walk through this alley, I notice the marks on the two walls seem to be conducting a conversation across time. The left wall belongs to a Republican-era brick building, its red bricks weathered to deep brown, the mortar between bricks having fallen away in places to reveal dark hollows inside. The right wall belongs to an 1980s concrete building, its surface coated with gradually peeling white lime, through which gray cement is faintly visible. Both walls bear water stain marks, but flowing in entirely different directions: the stains on the left wall meander downward, like an upside-down tree; the stains on the right wall expand horizontally, like a flat layer of clouds. I speculate this is because the two walls differ in material and surface roughness, causing rainwater to flow differently on each. At the narrowest point of the alley, each wall has a greenish patch — algae spawned by water splashing onto the walls during rainy pooling, facing each other across less than sixty centimeters, like two strangers who have never met yet grew identical features because of the same environment.

questions.q_zh: 左边墙上的水渍呈什么形状？
questions.q_en: What shape do the water stains on the left wall resemble?

options.pinyin: yì kē dào guà de shù

questions.q_zh: 巷子最窄处两面墙上的绿色区域是什么？
questions.q_en: What are the green areas on both walls at the narrowest point?

title: The Archaeology of a Drawer
text_zh: 整理书桌抽屉的时候，我突然意识到这个抽屉就像一个微型的考古遗址——越往下层翻，东西越古老。最上面一层是最近几天的物品：一支用了一半的笔、几张便签纸、一个充电线缠绕在一起的耳机。第二层开始出现一些遗忘已久的东西：一张三个月前的电影票根、一把不知道开什么锁的钥匙、一包已经过期的润喉糖。第三层几乎可以称为「文化层」了：一本两年前的小笔记本，里面记着当时学习外语的单词，字迹工整而陌生，仿佛出自另一个人之手。最底层是真正的「远古文物」——一张褪色的照片、一枚纪念币、一块已经干透的橡皮泥。每一件物品都标记着我生活中某个已经结束的阶段，它们沉入抽屉底部的方式和文物被泥土覆盖的过程惊人地相似：不是被刻意埋葬的，而是被新的日常生活一层一层地覆盖，直到被彻底遗忘。我小心翼翼地把每一件东西拿出来端详，然后又按照原来的顺序放了回去——就像一个考古学家在记录完遗址以后选择了原地保存。
text_pinyin: Zhěnglǐ shūzhuō chōuti de shíhou, wǒ tūrán yìshí dào zhège chōuti jiù xiàng yí gè wēixíng de kǎogǔ yízhǐ — yuè wǎng xià céng fān, dōngxi yuè gǔlǎo. Zuì shàngmiàn yì céng shì zuìjìn jǐ tiān de wùpǐn: yì zhī yòng le yíbàn de bǐ, jǐ zhāng biànqiān zhǐ, yí gè chōngdiàn xiàn chánrào zài yìqǐ de ěrjī. Dì èr céng kāishǐ chūxiàn yìxiē yíwàng yǐ jiǔ de dōngxi: yì zhāng sān gè yuè qián de diànyǐng piào gēn, yì bǎ bù zhīdào kāi shénme suǒ de yàoshi, yì bāo yǐjīng guòqī de rùnhóu táng. Dì sān céng jīhū kěyǐ chēng wéi 「wénhuà céng」 le: yì běn liǎng nián qián de xiǎo bǐjìběn, lǐmiàn jì zhe dāngshí xuéxí wàiyǔ de dāncí, zìjì gōngzhěng ér mòshēng, fǎngfú chūzì lìng yí gè rén zhī shǒu. Zuì dǐcéng shì zhēnzhèng de 「yuǎngǔ wénwù」 — yì zhāng tuìsè de zhàopiàn, yì méi jìniànbì, yí kuài yǐjīng gān tòu de xiàngpíní. Měi yí jiàn wùpǐn dōu biāojì zhe wǒ shēnghuó zhōng mǒu gè yǐjīng jiéshù de jiēduàn, tāmen chén rù chōuti dǐbù de fāngshì hé wénwù bèi nítǔ fùgài de guòchéng jīngrén de xiāngsì: bú shì bèi kèyì máizàng de, ér shì bèi xīn de rìcháng shēnghuó yì céng yì céng de fùgài, zhídào bèi chèdǐ yíwàng. Wǒ xiǎoxīnyìyì de bǎ měi yí jiàn dōngxi ná chūlái duānxiáng, ránhòu yòu ànzhào yuánlái de shùnxù fàng le huíqù — jiù xiàng yí gè kǎogǔ xuéjiā zài jìlù wán yízhǐ yǐhòu xuǎnzé le yuándì bǎocún.
text_en: While organizing my desk drawer, I suddenly realized this drawer is like a miniature archaeological site — the deeper you dig, the older things get. The top layer contains items from the last few days: a half-used pen, some sticky notes, earphones with charging cable tangled together. The second layer reveals long-forgotten things: a movie ticket stub from three months ago, a key that opens who-knows-what lock, a pack of already-expired throat lozenges. The third layer can almost be called a 'cultural stratum': a small notebook from two years ago with foreign language vocabulary written inside, the handwriting neat yet unfamiliar, as if from another person's hand. The bottom layer holds true 'ancient artifacts' — a faded photograph, a commemorative coin, a piece of completely dried-out modeling clay. Each item marks some already-concluded phase of my life, and the way they sank to the bottom of the drawer is strikingly similar to how artifacts are covered by soil: not deliberately buried, but covered layer by layer by new daily life until completely forgotten. I carefully take each item out to examine it, then put everything back in its original order — like an archaeologist who, after documenting a site, chooses to preserve it in place.

questions.q_zh: 抽屉第三层的笔记本里记着什么？
questions.q_en: What is recorded in the notebook on the third layer?

questions.q_zh: 作者最后怎么处理了抽屉里的东西？
questions.q_en: What did the author ultimately do with the drawer items?

options.text: 按照原来的顺序放了回去
options.pinyin: ànzhào yuánlái de shùnxù fàng le huíqù
options.text_en: Put them back in original order

options.pinyin: fàng dào le xīn de hézi lǐ
options.text_en: Put them in a new box
